thesis: AST SpaceMobile is pioneering the world's first space-based cellular broadband network, enabling standard smartphones to connect directly to satellites without specialized hardware. With Q1 2026 revenue of $14.7M, FY2026 guidance of $150M–$200M, a contracted backlog exceeding $1.2B, and partnerships with AT&T, Verizon, and 50+ global carriers covering ~3 billion potential subscribers, the company is at a critical inflection point. The stock has pulled back from $133.86 to ~$71.45, improving risk/reward for speculative investors with a 3–5 year horizon, though Q1 EPS miss and BlueBird 7 write-off raise near-term execution concerns.

drivers: Successful deployment of 45 BlueBird satellites by end of 2026 enabling continuous U.S. coverage
AT&T and Verizon commercial service launches in H2 2026 drive subscriber adoption above 5% of rural customers
International partnerships (Vodafone, Rakuten, stc) generate material revenue by 2027
Government contracts scale to $200M+ annually with 20–30% margins
Revenue grows from $150M–$200M (FY2026) to $2–$3B by 2028 with gross margins expanding to 60%+

base_case.assumptions: ASTS deploys 45 satellites and achieves continuous coverage by late 2026. Commercial service launches with mixed adoption—grows steadily but not explosively. SpaceX competition is real but ASTS maintains 30–40% market share through carrier partnerships. Revenue grows to $400–$600M by 2027 and $1.5B by 2029. Company reaches EBITDA breakeven by 2028–2029. Stock trades based on execution with multiple compressing to 50–80x sales as growth matures.

risks: SpaceX Starlink with T-Mobile proves 'good enough' for most users, capturing 70%+ market share
AT&T or Verizon switch allegiance to SpaceX or develop in-house solutions
Satellite launches face delays or technical issues; constellation incomplete by 2027
Revenue growth stalls below $150M in FY2026; cash burn forces highly dilutive financing
Market loses patience with 'story stocks'; multiple compression to 30x sales or lower

catalysts.event: BlueBird satellite launches targeting 45 in orbit
Q2 2026 earnings — revenue acceleration vs. Q1 is critical
Constellation completion milestone — 45 satellites in orbit
Government contract awards (DoD, Space Development Agency, Golden Dome)

key_risks: SpaceX Starlink competition with deeper pockets, lower launch costs, and T-Mobile partnership
BlueBird 7 write-off of $155M–$160M raises manufacturing and execution risks
Q1 2026 EPS miss (-$0.66 vs. -$0.21 est.) shows larger-than-expected losses
Heavy customer concentration with AT&T and Verizon; loss of either partner would collapse revenue
Need for 5–7x revenue growth to reach profitability; FY2026 guidance of $150M–$200M is still far from breakeven
Convertible note overhang creates potential future dilution of 15–30%
High cash burn rate (~$150M+ per quarter normalized) requires disciplined execution
